017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view.thymeleaf;
018
019import java.io.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
022import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.processor.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view.*;
025import org.thymeleaf.*;
026import org.thymeleaf.context.*;
027
028/**
029 * {@link ResponseProcessor} that detects {@link ThymeleafView}-typed return values and asks the
030 * configured {@link TemplateEngine} to render them directly onto the response writer.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * Auto-registered by {@link ThymeleafMixin} via
034 * {@link Rest#responseProcessors() @Rest(responseProcessors=...)}
035 * &mdash; callers who add the mixin don't need to wire up this class explicitly. Callers who want
036 * to handle {@code ThymeleafView} returns <i>without</i> adopting the mixin can add this class to
037 * their own
038 * {@link Rest#responseProcessors() responseProcessors} list.
039 *
040 * <h5 class='section'>Behavior:</h5>
041 *
042 * <ol class='spaced-list'>
043 *    <li>Inspect the response content. If the value is not a {@link ThymeleafView}, return
044 *       {@link ResponseProcessor#NEXT NEXT} so the rest of the chain runs.
045 *    <li>Read the active {@link ThymeleafMixin} from the {@code RestContext} bean store to
046 *       discover the {@link TemplateEngine} (lazy default if no engine bean is registered).
047 *    <li>Build a Thymeleaf {@link Context} populated with the view's attribute map.
048 *    <li>Apply every entry from {@link ThymeleafView#getResponseHeaders()} via
049 *       {@link jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse#setHeader(String, String)
050 *       res.setHeader(...)}.
051 *    <li>Default {@code Content-Type} to {@code text/html;charset=UTF-8} if the caller did not set
052 *       one explicitly (Thymeleaf's HTML template mode emits HTML; an explicit caller header wins).
053 *    <li>Call {@code templateEngine.process(view.getTemplateName(), context, res.getWriter())} to
054 *       stream the rendered output.
055 *    <li>When no Thymeleaf engine is on the classpath, the {@code TemplateEngine}-typed import here
056 *       fails to load at first use and surfaces {@link #NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC} naming the missing
057 *       dependency.
058 * </ol>
059 *
060 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
061 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ThymeleafMixin}
062 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ThymeleafView}
063 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ResponseProcessors">Response Processors</a>
064 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ThymeleafViewSupport">Thymeleaf View Support</a>
065 * </ul>
066 *
067 * @since 10.0.0
068 */
069@SuppressWarnings({
070   "resource" // Closeables here are framework-managed and not owned/closed by this class; not a real leak.
071})
072public class ThymeleafViewRenderer implements ViewRenderer {
073
074   /** Default {@code Content-Type} applied when the view does not specify one explicitly. */
075   public static final String DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE = "text/html;charset=UTF-8";
076
077   /**
078    * Diagnostic message emitted when no Thymeleaf engine can be found on the classpath at
079    * render-time.
080    *
081    * <p>
082    * Public so tests and consumer apps can pattern-match against the message text without
083    * depending on internal string literals.
084    */
085   public static final String NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC = """
086      No Thymeleaf engine is available on the classpath. Add one of:
087        - org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-thymeleaf  (Spring Boot autoconfig)
088        - org.thymeleaf:thymeleaf                                  (Juneau microservice / Jetty)
089      Or register a custom @Bean TemplateEngine that picks up your preferred resolvers.
090      See https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ThymeleafViewSupport for the full matrix.""";
091
092   @Override /* Overridden from ResponseProcessor */
093   public int process(RestOpSession opSession) throws IOException, BasicHttpException {
094      var req = opSession.getRequest();
095      var res = opSession.getResponse();
096
097      var content = res.getContent(Object.class);
098      if (! (content instanceof ThymeleafView view))
099         return NEXT;
100
101      // Resolve the bridge resource (carries TemplateEngine + cached default). Fall back to a
102      // fresh ThymeleafMixin when the renderer is used standalone without the mixin.
103      var bridge = req.getContext().getBeanStore()
104         .getBean(ThymeleafMixin.class)
105         .orElseGet(ThymeleafMixin::new);
106
107      // Apply caller-supplied response headers first so a caller-provided Content-Type wins
108      // over the bridge's default below.
109      view.getResponseHeaders().forEach(res::setHeader);
110      if (! res.containsHeader("Content-Type"))
111         res.setHeader("Content-Type", DEFAULT_CONTENT_TYPE);
112
113      try {
114         var engine = bridge.resolveTemplateEngine(req);
115         var ctx = newContext(req, view);
116         engine.process(view.getTemplateName(), ctx, res.getWriter());
117         return FINISHED;
118      } catch (LinkageError ex) {
119         throw new InternalServerError(ex, NO_ENGINE_DIAGNOSTIC);
120      } catch (IOException ex) {
121         throw ex;
122      } catch (Exception ex) {
123         throw new InternalServerError(ex, "Thymeleaf render failed for ''{0}''",
124            view.getTemplateName());
125      }
126   }
127
128   /**
129    * Builds a fresh Thymeleaf {@link Context} for the current request, populated with every
130    * entry from {@link ThymeleafView#getAttributes()}.
131    *
132    * <p>
133    * The {@code Context} inherits the request's {@link java.util.Locale Locale} so Thymeleaf's
134    * i18n machinery (`#messages`, `MessageSource`) picks it up automatically.
135    *
136    * @param req The current REST request.
137    * @param view The view being rendered.
138    * @return A new {@link Context} carrying the view's attributes.
139    */
140   static Context newContext(RestRequest req, ThymeleafView view) {
141      var ctx = new Context(req.getLocale());
142      view.getAttributes().forEach(ctx::setVariable);
143      return ctx;
144   }
145}
